usage_instructions: Use `airtable auth login` first to configure credentials for the default or named profile, then use `tables list`, `tables get`, `fields list`, `fields get`, and `records list` against the configured base. Base ID defaults to `AIRTABLE_BASE_ID`; override with `--base/-b` when the target base matters. Table-name arguments are Airtable table names inside the selected base, not project CLI resource names such as CourseCraft `modules`.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management for Airtable. Use `auth login` to configure credentials, `auth status` for a quick profile-level check, `auth test` to verify stored credentials work against the live API, `auth profiles` to manage multiple saved profiles, and `auth logout` to clear credentials.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure Airtable credentials for the current or named profile. Use --credential-type/-c to re-enter only one credential type, --profile/-p to write to a specific profile, and --force/-F to clear stored credentials before reauthenticating.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored Airtable credentials. Use --profile/-p to clear a specific profile's credentials.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management for multiple Airtable accounts or environments. Use to create, list, switch, or delete profiles. One profile is set as default at a time.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile with NAME (required positional argument). Creates from .env.example template.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name (e.g., staging, production)

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and all its data by NAME (required positional argument). Prompts for confirmation unless --force/-F is used.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to delete

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a single profile by NAME (required positional argument). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all configured Airtable profiles. Shows which is default. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --filter/-f to find specific profiles (e.g., is_default:eq:True).

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to delete

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Check current auth status without making API calls. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test that stored credentials actually work by making an API call. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

get.help: Get one base by ID or name from the accessible bases list.

arguments.name: base_id
arguments.help: The base ID (beginning with app) or base name

list.help: List every base the active Personal Access Token can access.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

cache.usage_instructions: Manage the local Airtable CLI response cache.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Clear all cached Airtable CLI responses. Use when stale cached API data is affecting command output.

fields.usage_instructions: Schema field operations for Airtable tables. Use `fields list` to enumerate a table schema, `fields get` to inspect one field, `fields create` to add a new field, and `fields update` to change a field name, description, or type-specific options. Airtable's public Web/Meta API does not support deleting fields; use the Airtable web UI for field deletion, then verify removal with fields get/list. Write operations require a token with `schema.bases:write`.

create.help: Create a field in an Airtable table.
create.usage_instructions: Create a field in TABLE_ID with NAME and FIELD_TYPE positional arguments. Use --description/-d for a field description, --options/-o with a JSON object for type-specific options, --base/-b to override BASE_ID, and --table/-t for table output.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID, beginning with tbl

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: The field name

arguments.name: field_type
arguments.help: The Airtable field type, such as singleLineText

options.name: --options
options.help: Field options as a JSON object

delete.help: Report that field deletion is not supported by Airtable's public API.
delete.usage_instructions: This command is guarded because Airtable's public Web/Meta API does not support deleting fields. It returns an unsupported-operation error before making any API request. Delete the field in Airtable's web UI, then verify removal with `airtable fields get <table> <field>` or `airtable fields list <table> --filter 'name:eq:<field name>'`.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID, beginning with tbl

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: The field ID, beginning with fld

get.help: Get a single field schema by ID or name.
get.usage_instructions: Get one field definition by TABLE_ID and FIELD_ID. TABLE_ID may be a table ID or Airtable table name in the selected base; FIELD_ID may be a field ID or field name. Use --base/-b to override AIRTABLE_BASE_ID and --table/-t for tabular output. Do not pass project CLI resource names unless they are also real Airtable table names in that base.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or table name

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: The field ID or field name

list.help: List fields in an Airtable table.
list.usage_instructions: List schema fields for TABLE_ID, which may be a table ID or Airtable table name in the selected base. Use --base/-b to override AIRTABLE_BASE_ID, --limit/-l to cap results, --filter/-f with field:op:value expressions such as name:eq:Status or type:eq:singleLineText, --properties/-p to project specific keys, and --table/-t for tabular output. Do not pass project CLI resource names unless they are also real Airtable table names in that base.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or table name

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

update.help: Update a field's name, description, or options.
examples: airtable fields update tblXXXXXXXXXXXXXX fldXXXXXXXXXXXXXX --name "New Status"
update.usage_instructions: Update FIELD_ID in TABLE_ID. Specify at least one of --name/-n, --description/-d, or --options/-o; --options must be a JSON object. Use --base/-b to override BASE_ID and --table/-t for table output.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID, beginning with tbl

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: The field ID, beginning with fld

options.name: --options
options.help: Field options as a JSON object

records.usage_instructions: CRUD operations on Airtable records. All record commands require TABLE_ID (name or ID) as first positional argument. Base ID defaults to .env but can be overridden with --base/-b. Field values for create/update use 'FieldName=value' pairs.

create.usage_instructions: Create a new record. TABLE_ID is required positional, followed by FIELD_VALUES as 'FieldName=value' pairs. Use --typecast to enable automatic type conversion. Use --base/-b to override default base.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or name

arguments.name: field_values
arguments.help: FIELD_VALUES...  Field values as 'FieldName=value' pairs

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a record. TABLE_ID and RECORD_ID are required positional args. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes/-y is used. Use --base/-b to override default base.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or name

arguments.name: record_id
arguments.help: The record ID

get.help: Get a specific record by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get a single record by TABLE_ID and RECORD_ID (both required positional). Use --base/-b to override default base. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or name

arguments.name: record_id
arguments.help: The record ID

list.help: List records from an Airtable table.
list.usage_instructions: List records from a table. TABLE_ID is required (positional). Use --base/-b to override default base. Use --filter/-f for field filtering (e.g., Status:eq:Active) or --formula for raw Airtable formulas. Use --sort-field and --sort-direction for ordering. Use --view to filter by view. Default limit is 100. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or name

options.name: --base
options.help: The base ID (defaults to BASE_ID in .env)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

options.name: --view
options.help: View name or ID to filter

update.help: Update a record's fields. Use PATCH (default) to update only specified fields, or --replace for PUT to clear unspecified fields.
update.usage_instructions: Update a record. TABLE_ID and RECORD_ID are required positional args, followed by FIELD_VALUES as 'FieldName=value' pairs. Default is PATCH (partial update); use --replace for PUT (clears unspecified fields). Use --typecast to enable automatic type conversion.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID or name

arguments.name: record_id
arguments.help: The record ID

arguments.name: field_values
arguments.help: FIELD_VALUES...  Field updates as 'FieldName=value' pairs

options.name: --replace
options.help: Replace entire record (PUT) instead of updating (PATCH)

arguments.name: record_id
arguments.help: The record ID to attach the file to

arguments.name: field_name
arguments.help: Attachment field ID or name

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.help: Path to the local file to upload

options.name: --content-type
options.help: MIME type override (auto-detected from filename when omitted)

tables.usage_instructions: Table-level schema operations for an Airtable base. Use `tables list` to enumerate all tables in a base, `tables get` to inspect one table schema, `tables create` to create a new table, and `tables update` to rename a table or change its description. The Meta API requires at least one field on create; if --fields is omitted, a single primary singleLineText field named "Name" is added automatically. Base ID defaults to AIRTABLE_BASE_ID but can be overridden with --base/-b. Airtable does not expose a public delete-table endpoint, so this command group has no `delete` action.

create.help: Create a new table in an Airtable base. The Meta API requires at least one field; if --fields is omitted, a single primary field named "Name" of type singleLineText is created.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new table in a base. NAME is the table name (required positional). --description/-d sets a description (max 20,000 chars). --fields takes a JSON array of {name, type, options?, description?} field objects; the first entry becomes the primary field and must be a supported primary field type. If --fields is omitted, a default `[{"name":"Name","type":"singleLineText"}]` is used. --base/-b overrides AIRTABLE_BASE_ID. --table/-t for tabular output. Requires schema.bases:write.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: The new table name

options.name: --fields
options.help: Fields as a JSON array of {name, type, ...} objects. Defaults to a single primary 'Name' singleLineText field if omitted

get.help: Get one table schema by ID or name.
get.usage_instructions: Get one table schema by TABLE_ID, which may be either a table ID or a table name. Use --base/-b to override AIRTABLE_BASE_ID and --table/-t for tabular output.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID (beginning with tbl) or table name

list.help: List tables in an Airtable base.
list.usage_instructions: List every table in a base. Use --base/-b to override AIRTABLE_BASE_ID, --table/-t for a tabular display, --filter/-f with field:op:value (e.g., name:contains:log), --limit/-l to cap results, and --properties/-p to project specific keys. JSON output is a summary list with `id`, `name`, `primaryFieldId`, `description`, and `field_count`; use `tables get` for the full schema including fields and views.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

update.help: Update a table's name or description.
update.usage_instructions: Rename a table or update its description. TABLE_ID may be a table ID (tbl...) or a table name. Specify at least one of --name/-n or --description/-d. --base/-b overrides AIRTABLE_BASE_ID. --table/-t for tabular output. Requires schema.bases:write.

arguments.name: table_id
arguments.help: The table ID (beginning with tbl) or table name
